text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. The main goal is to make your code readable and easy to understand for yourself and others. One of the easiest ways to achieve this is by using clear naming conventions. For example, a variable called `userAge` is much more meaningful than `x`. Good names help anyone reading the code to quickly grasp its purpose. Another key practice is to keep your functions short and focused on a single task. This improves maintainability because you can find and fix problems more easily. In short, clean code saves time and reduces frustration in the long run.
